content: Reflexion’s dynamic persistent memory acts as a "long-term self-reflection journal" for Self-Refine’s iterative local feedback loop, turning disconnected one-off output improvements into cumulative capability growth that accumulates across tasks rather than resetting for every new prompt. This combination solves a key unaddressed limitation of vanilla Self-Refine: it retains the framework’s core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision while enabling long-tail improvement that compounds over time.
